_comment: S7 Compounding / co-channel overlap — recovery under signals that physically sum in one slot at a single receiver (STUDY-SPEC §6). Each part lists explicit per-signal (msg_id, freq_hz, dt_s, snr_db). The harness renders each station clean, scales it by its relative snr_db (so snr_db sets station STRENGTH — capture pairs really differ in level), sums them, and adds ONE shared band-noise floor. Truth is logged one row PER SIGNAL so the existing matcher scores each compounded message independently. 2026-06-19 (R1): All exactly-co-frequency (Δ0 Hz) parts revised to use realistic ~10 Hz frequency offsets. 2026-06-19 (R2): Uniform Δ10 Hz across co_channel, time_freq and capture families replaced with varied offsets (Δ7–Δ14 Hz, non-multiples of 10) — a uniform round-number offset is no more representative of real-world click dispersion than Δ0 Hz. The near_collision family (parts 3–7) retains its specific separation probe points (3, 6, 12, 25, 50 Hz) unchanged. Results from R1 runs (uniform Δ10 Hz) are not directly comparable to R2 runs.
name: Compounding / co-channel overlap
response: Decoded / not-decoded per compounded message (attribute, per-signal recovery)
description: 4 overlap families × 10 trials. Drives: per-message recovery and between-app agreement when 2–3 signals occupy the same or near-same audio frequency / time slot — the real-world pileup case that S4 (frequency-spread QRM) never exercises. Revised 2026-06-19 R2: co_channel, time_freq and capture families now use varied, irregular inter-signal frequency offsets (Δ7–Δ14 Hz) rather than a uniform Δ10 Hz repeated across all parts. The near_collision family retains its specific separation probe points.
harness_note: Rendered by _render_compound via channel.mix_to_shared_floor: each signal is encoded clean, scaled by 10^(snr_db/20) (relative strength vs the shared floor), summed, then a single seeded AWGN floor is added once for the whole slot (NOT one floor per signal). One truth row is written per signal, carrying that signal's true freq/dt/snr (snr = strength vs the shared floor), so the matcher's text+freq branch scores each message on its own.

parts.label: 2-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ7 Hz
parts.note: Two stations targeting the same channel with a 7 Hz tuning offset — a small but realistic miss when operators independently click the same waterfall spot. Revised from Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: 2-stack, equal -5 dB, Δ13 Hz
parts.note: Co-channel pair near the decode floor with a 13 Hz tuning offset — a slightly wider click miss, still well within the FT8 50 Hz channel bandwidth. Revised from Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: 3-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ8 / Δ11 Hz asymmetric
parts.note: Triple co-channel pileup with asymmetric per-station offsets — three operators independently clicking ~1500 Hz, landing 8 Hz below, on-frequency, and 11 Hz above. Asymmetry is intentional: three independent operators will not produce a symmetric spread. Revised from symmetric Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: delta 3 Hz
parts.note: Sub-bin separation (< one 6.25 Hz tone bin).

parts.label: delta 6 Hz
parts.note: ~one tone bin apart.

parts.label: delta 12 Hz
parts.note: ~two tone bins apart.

parts.label: delta 25 Hz
parts.note: Partial spectral overlap (FT8 occupies 50 Hz).

parts.label: delta 50 Hz
parts.note: Edge-to-edge — one full FT8 bandwidth apart; expected to separate cleanly.

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ8 Hz, dt 0.0 / 0.5 s
parts.note: Near-co-frequency pair with 8 Hz offset, late replier offset by 0.5 s. Revised from Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ11 Hz, dt 0.0 / 1.0 s
parts.note: Near-co-frequency pair with 11 Hz offset, 1.0 s time stagger. Revised from Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ9 Hz, dt 0.0 / 2.0 s
parts.note: Near-co-frequency pair with 9 Hz offset, 2.0 s time stagger (near sync-window edge). Revised from Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ14 Hz, 0 / -3 dB
parts.note: Capture effect: 3 dB stronger vs weaker, 14 Hz frequency offset. Revised from Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ9 Hz, 0 / -6 dB
parts.note: 6 dB capture margin with 9 Hz frequency offset. Revised from Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ7 Hz, 0 / -10 dB
parts.note: 10 dB capture margin — weaker likely suppressed; 7 Hz frequency offset. Revised from Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: near-co-freq Δ11 Hz, +3 / -10 dB
parts.note: Strong dominant signal over a weak one; 11 Hz frequency offset. Revised from Δ10 Hz (2026-06-19 R2).

parts.label: offset-sweep: 2-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ5 Hz
parts.note: Offset sensitivity sweep point. 2-stack equal-SNR at 5 Hz separation — sub-bin (< one 6.25 Hz tone bin); expected to be the hardest separation. Added 2026-06-19. Run with --parts 15,16,17,18.

parts.label: offset-sweep: 2-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ7 Hz
parts.note: Offset sensitivity sweep point. 2-stack equal-SNR at 7 Hz separation — mirrors R2 P0 (same frequencies, 1500/1507) but on independent seeds (different part_index). R2 P0 result for comparison: 13/20 (65%). Added 2026-06-19.

parts.label: offset-sweep: 2-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ10 Hz
parts.note: Offset sensitivity sweep point. 2-stack equal-SNR at 10 Hz separation — mirrors R1 P0 (1500/1510) but on independent seeds. R1 P0 result for comparison: 20/20 (100%). Added 2026-06-19.

parts.label: offset-sweep: 2-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ15 Hz
parts.note: Offset sensitivity sweep point. 2-stack equal-SNR at 15 Hz separation — ~2.4 tone bins apart; expected to be easier to separate than Δ10 Hz. Added 2026-06-19.

parts.label: offset-sweep: 2-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ8 Hz
parts.note: Threshold-narrowing sweep point. 2-stack equal-SNR at 8 Hz separation — between confirmed gap (Δ7 Hz, OpenWSFZ 70%) and confirmed parity (Δ10 Hz, 100%). Added 2026-06-19 to locate the performance cliff. Run with --parts 19,20.

parts.label: offset-sweep: 2-stack, equal 0 dB, Δ9 Hz
parts.note: Threshold-narrowing sweep point. 2-stack equal-SNR at 9 Hz separation — between confirmed gap (Δ7 Hz, OpenWSFZ 70%) and confirmed parity (Δ10 Hz, 100%). Added 2026-06-19 to locate the performance cliff. Run with --parts 19,20.

trials_note: K=10 per part. At 10 trials the co_channel family yields ~60 binary observations, giving a minimum detectable improvement of ~10% at α=0.05 and per-part resolution of ±16%. May be reduced back to 3 for routine regression gates once a stable baseline has been established under the varied-offset scenario.
